So far, it still says "Use Oxford spelling". Some examples listed below (I'm only including languages that I'm at least moderately conversant in, maybe other editors can look for sources in other languages) *English (US): [https://edition.cnn.com/2026/03/04/middleeast/us-israel-iran-war-what-we-know-intl-hnk CNN] calls it 'Middle East war', [https://www.reuters.com/world/europe/france-send-armored-vehicles-lebanon-macron-says-2026-03-05/ Reuters] calling it 'war in the Middle East', [https://www.pbs.org/newshour/world/war-in-middle-east-widens-as-israeli-and-u-s-planes-pound-iran-and-tehran-and-its-proxies-hit-back PBS] *English (UK): [https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/mar/02/what-is-uk-involvement-middle-east-war-us-israel-iran The Guardian] calls it 'Middle East war', [https://www.independent.co.uk/news/world/middle-east/iran-war-israel-trump-middle-east-b2930268.html The Independent] in the first sentence says 'The war in the Middle East...' *English (Ireland): [https://www.irishtimes.com/world/middle-east/2026/03/02/in-maps-how-war-on-iran-is-spreading-across-middle-east/ Irish Times], the headline is 'Five days of war across Middle East' *German: Can't link directly, but the headline on the homepage is "Krieg in Nahost" or "War in Middle East" [https://www.tagesschau.de/ Tagesschau] *Dutch: [https://nos.nl/collectie/14015-oorlog-midden-oosten NOS] *French: [https://www.lemonde.fr/guerre-au-proche-orient/ Le Mond] *Spanish: [https://www.elpais.com.uy/mundo/estados-unidos/que-pasa-en-la-guerra-en-medio-oriente-claves-para-entender-la-escalada-el-impacto-y-la-situacion-actual El Pais] --[[User:ScrubbedFalcon|ScrubbedFalcon]] ([[User talk:ScrubbedFalcon|talk]]) 18:38, 5 March 2026 (UTC) :@[[User:ScrubbedFalcon|ScrubbedFalcon]] give it a few more days. But a couple things: one headline at an outlet doesn't even demonstrate a common name at that outlet, only what's in use in a particular headline. ''The Guardian'', for example, is referring to both a "Middle East crisis" and "US-Israel war on Iran" in headers (and directly under the linked headline) on their website (the dual use may reflect a question of scope which is not necessarily best answered in a discussion about common name for this conflict). Foreign language sources don't contribute to [[WP:COMMONNAME]]. —[[User:BrechtBro|BrechtBro]] [[User talk:BrechtBro|(talk)]] 19:17, 5 March 2026 (UTC) == Status of the Republic of Lebanon? == The Lebanese Republic has been acting against Hezbollah using its army and law-enforcement procedures, arresting various individuals and partly cooperating with Israel. Considering the fact other countries are even supplying their anti-militant attitude (with France even offering armored vehicles to Lebanon to persue their goals:https://aje.news/0avpno?update=4369419) '''''Should the position of Lebanon be shown in any way in the infobox?''''' [[User:VitoxxMass|VitoxxMass]] ([[User talk:VitoxxMass|talk]]) 18:50, 5 March 2026 (UTC) == [[WP:TE]]: Iran's "destruction of Israel" rhetoric == @[[User:Ruzhiner|Ruzhiner]], you have added material to the background section at least three times ([https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=2026_Iran_war&diff=prev&oldid=1341283310 1], [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=2026_Iran_war&diff=prev&oldid=1341655922 2], [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=2026_Iran_war&diff=prev&oldid=1341864959 3]) concerning the Iranian government's rhetoric around the "destruction of Israel as a Jewish state". I explained in a [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Talk:2026_Iran_war&diff=prev&oldid=1341362488 prior talk page discussion] that I consider this material to be undue in this section of the article, which should focus on the material relations between belligerents prior to the conflict. As I explained in my previous comment, {{tq|both sides of this conflict are anti-each other and have called for one another's destruction in a variety of ways. It would be more concise and neutral to simply state that Iran has led an [[Axis of Resistance]]...}} Focusing on actions instead of rhetoric ensures that the material in the background section is relevant and unbiased. Rhetoric and its interpretation is highly contentious and subjective, but opposing sources can more often agree on material facts. Furthermore, I find the sourcing you've used to support this material to be insufficient. While relatively recent, it does not directly connect Iranian rhetoric to this war as a cause of it, nor do any of the post-2/28 sources I've read. Age concerns aside, the source you've used is highly polemical, seemingly exclusively blaming Iran for the entire decades-long conflict with Israel. That's one perspective on things to be sure, but it's a contentious perspective that doesn't belong in a section where we don't have the space to get into contested claims. Finally, [https://nps.edu/web/iris/-/afshon-ostovar-ph-d- the author] is employed by the [[Naval Postgraduate School]] -- a belligerent in the conflict. He is therefore not fit to serve as a representative of independent opinion on the causes of the war and its prelude. For these reasons, I am removing the material. Please engage in discussion and seek consensus if you wish to advocate further for the inclusion of this content. [[User:Monk of Monk Hall|Monk of Monk Hall]] ([[User talk:Monk of Monk Hall|talk]]) 20:01, 5 March 2026 (UTC) :@[[User:Monk of Monk Hall|Monk of Monk Hall]] I would add that the essential information is also conveyed by "...adopted a hostile posture towards the US and Israel." which is much more concise, and that the link to Iran-Israel relations there is not a very useful navigational aid, considering all the othee context. —[[User:BrechtBro|BrechtBro]] [[User talk:BrechtBro|(talk)]] 20:23, 5 March 2026 (UTC) == Infobox: "Attacked by Israel: Lebanon" == Hezbollah has attacked Israel on 2 March,[http://bbc.com/news/articles/c4gq3ykg7pvo] and Israel retaliated with an operation against Hezbollah, in cooperation with the Lebanese government.[https://www.wsj.com/livecoverage/iran-israel-us-strikes-2026/card/lebanon-s-prime-minister-bans-hezbollah-military-activities-wY2WSscG79E5Ibdk6zkK] Israel has not attacked the Lebanese state. This should be removed from the infobox.
